exam.name: New York Real Estate Salesperson
exam.category_description: Master property laws, contracts, and ethical practices needed to pass your real estate licensing exams and confidently represent clients.
exam.hero_headline: Pass the NY Real Estate Salesperson exam with confidence.
exam.hero_subheadline: Exam-aligned property and contract study paths built from the official NY DOS syllabus and designed for long-term retention.
exam.companion_problem: Most prep material is dry, overwhelming, or disconnected from the exact NY DOS curriculum.
exam.companion_solution: Only Ever maps the official syllabus into structured 15-minute topics and retention-first notes so you learn exactly what the exam expects.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring real estate professional in New York. Navigating property laws, agency rules, and real estate finance. Responds well to practical real estate scenarios, closing procedures, and client interaction examples.

sections.title: License Law and Regulations
sections.description: Introduction to the laws and regulations governing real estate licensure in New York.

topics.title: Introduction to Licensing Law
topics.description: Understand the purpose of the licensing law and the different categories of licensure.
learning_objectives: Explain the fundamental purpose of real estate licensing laws in New York.
Differentiate between the roles of a Real Estate Broker, Associate Broker, and Salesperson.

topics.title: Responsibilities of Licensure
topics.description: Responsibilities owed by real estate brokers and salespersons to the public and to each other.
learning_objectives: Identify the broker's responsibilities to the public and to their licensees.
Understand the salesperson's obligations to the sponsoring broker and to the public.

topics.title: Education and Exemptions
topics.description: Education requirements to obtain a license, duties requiring licensure, and exemptions.
learning_objectives: List the specific education requirements for obtaining a New York real estate license.
Determine which activities require licensure and who is exempt from these rules.

topics.title: Maintaining a License
topics.description: Key requirements for maintaining an active real estate license in New York.
learning_objectives: Explain rules regarding who physically holds the license and the carrying of pocket cards.
Outline the steps to properly record changes in name, status, or location.

topics.title: Changes, Renewals, and Dual Licensure
topics.description: Procedures for changing associations, renewing licenses, and holding dual licenses.
learning_objectives: Describe the continuing education hours and renewal requirements for real estate agents.
Understand the procedures and implications of dual licensure as a broker and salesperson.

topics.title: Department of State Regulations
topics.description: Regulations promulgated by the Department of State, advertisement guidelines, and common violations.
learning_objectives: Identify the specific advertising guidelines mandated by the Department of State.
Recognize the most common violations of real estate license law.

topics.title: Required Property Disclosures
topics.description: Understanding Article 14 (Property Condition Disclosure Act) and uncapped natural gas wells disclosure.
learning_objectives: Explain the requirements of the Property Condition Disclosure Act under Article 14.
Describe the required disclosures regarding uncapped natural gas wells under Section 242 of the Real Property Law.

topics.title: Disciplinary Proceedings
topics.description: Penalties for violations and case studies involving disciplinary proceedings by the Department of State.
learning_objectives: Identify potential liabilities and penalties for violating license laws.
Analyze case studies to understand the outcomes of real estate disciplinary proceedings.

sections.title: Law of Agency
sections.description: Comprehensive understanding of agency relationships, fiduciary duties, and disclosure laws.

topics.title: The Real Estate Agent
topics.description: Licensure requirements and definitions regarding who the agent represents.
learning_objectives: Define the concept of agency in real estate.
Distinguish between a client and a customer in an agency relationship.

topics.title: The Agent as Fiduciary
topics.description: Fiduciary responsibilities and the scope of an agent's authority.
learning_objectives: Detail the fiduciary duties of loyalty, confidentiality, full disclosure, obedience, reasonable skill/care, and accounting.
Explain the consequences of agent misrepresentation.

topics.title: Agency and Brokerage Roles
topics.description: The roles of subagents, cooperating brokers, and buyer brokers.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between the roles of the principal agent and the subagent.
Explain the nature of cooperating brokers and their function in a transaction.

topics.title: Creation of Agency
topics.description: How an agency is created, compensation determination, and implied agency.
learning_objectives: Describe how an agency relationship is formally created.
Understand concepts like implied agency, ratification, and estoppel.

topics.title: Anti-Trust Issues
topics.description: Common anti-trust violations in real estate brokerage.
learning_objectives: Identify anti-trust violations including group boycotting and price fixing.
Explain market allocation agreements and tie-in arrangements.

topics.title: Agency Alternatives
topics.description: An overview of different types of agents in a real estate transaction.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between a seller's agent, buyer's agent, broker's agent, and dual agent.
Explain the role of a landlord's agent versus a tenant's agent.

topics.title: Subagency
topics.description: The function and creation of subagency and the obligations to buyers and sellers.
learning_objectives: Determine who creates subagency and the relationship to the principal.
Outline the specific obligations a subagent owes to the seller and to the buyer.

topics.title: Dual Agency Basics
topics.description: The definition of dual agency, informed consent, and associated risks.
learning_objectives: Define dual agency and the obligation to obtain informed consent from all parties.
Explain the risks and consequences of undisclosed dual agency.

topics.title: Consensual Dual Agency
topics.description: Consensual dual agency operations, designated agents, and New York regulatory schemes.
learning_objectives: Explain the difference between single licensee dual agency and broker dual agency.
Describe the process of establishing designated sales agents under company policy.

topics.title: Single Agency Relationships
topics.description: Practicing single agency, handling in-house vs. cooperative sales, and buyer brokerage.
learning_objectives: Summarize the principle that a single agency broker represents either the buyer or the seller, but never both.
Analyze the dynamics of buyer/tenant agency and who pays the buyer's broker.

topics.title: Termination of Agency
topics.description: How agency relationships are legally terminated.
learning_objectives: List the various ways an agency relationship can be terminated.
Understand the enduring fiduciary duties post-termination.

topics.title: Forms and Disclosure Policy
topics.description: Required forms, listing agreements, and section 443 of the Real Property Law.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between exclusive right to sell, exclusive agency, and open listing agreements.
Explain the disclosure requirements under Section 443 of the New York Real Property Law.

topics.title: Completing the Signature Page
topics.description: Handling the first substantive meeting and managing refusal to sign.
learning_objectives: Define 'first substantive meeting' and understand its requirements.
Detail the procedure if a buyer or seller refuses to sign the agency disclosure document.

topics.title: Independent Contractor Relationship
topics.description: Nature of the independent contractor relationship under common law and IRS rules.
learning_objectives: Explain the nature of the independent contractor relationship versus an employee.
Identify the criteria established by case law, the IRS, and New York State.

topics.title: 1986 Independent Contractor Laws
topics.description: New York State Labor Law additions and Internal Revenue Code Section 3508 compliance.
learning_objectives: Describe the 1986 additions to the New York State Labor Law.
Understand the requirements for a written contract not executed under duress.

topics.title: Compliance and Elements
topics.description: Specific elements that validate an independent contractor status.
learning_objectives: List the elements of an independent contractor relationship, including commission-based pay and autonomy over hours.
Explain why written documentation cannot overcome a broker's actual direction and control.

topics.title: Consequences of Non-Compliance
topics.description: Risks to brokers and salespersons if independent contractor laws are violated.
learning_objectives: Identify the financial and legal responsibilities placed on a broker if sales associates are deemed employees.
Explain the tax consequences for salespersons upon non-compliance.

sections.title: Legal Issues
sections.description: Estates, interests, liens, easements, deeds, and the title closing process.

topics.title: Estates and Interests Overview
topics.description: Introduction to the bundle of rights and property distinctions.
learning_objectives: Define the bundle of rights associated with property ownership.
Differentiate between land, real estate, real property, and personal property.

topics.title: Uses of Real Property
topics.description: Different uses and defining characteristics of real property.
learning_objectives: Identify various uses of real property, such as residential, commercial, industrial, and agricultural.
Understand the inherent characteristics of real property.

topics.title: Freehold and Leasehold Estates
topics.description: Understanding fee simple, life estates, and leasehold estate types.
learning_objectives: Define freehold estates including fee simple, qualified fee, and life estates.
Explain leasehold estates such as estate for years, periodic estate, estate at will, and estate at sufferance.

topics.title: Forms of Ownership
topics.description: Estate in severalty and types of co-ownership.
learning_objectives: Describe estate in severalty and types of co-ownership like tenancy in common and joint tenancy.
Explain the concept of tenancy by the entirety and the termination of joint tenancies.

topics.title: Business Organizations and Ownership
topics.description: Trusts, partnerships, corporations, and syndicates.
learning_objectives: Understand how partnerships, corporations, and syndicates own real estate.
Identify the nature of property interests transferred in cooperatives and condominiums.

topics.title: Encumbrances and Liens
topics.description: Voluntary and involuntary liens, and general liens.
learning_objectives: Define encumbrances and distinguish between voluntary and involuntary liens.
Provide examples of general liens such as judgments and estate taxes.

topics.title: Specific Liens
topics.description: Tax liens, mortgage liens, mechanic's liens, and priority of liens.
learning_objectives: Identify specific liens and understand their effect on title.
Explain the priority of liens and the function of subordination agreements.

topics.title: Easements and Deed Restrictions
topics.description: Types of easements, creation, termination, and deed restrictions.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between easement appurtenant, easement in gross, necessity, and prescription.
Describe the process for creating and terminating an easement.

topics.title: Definition and Purpose of Deeds
topics.description: The function and significance of deeds in real estate transactions.
learning_objectives: Define a deed and explain its overarching purpose.
Understand the concepts of metes and bounds, monuments, and block description.

topics.title: Essential Elements of a Deed
topics.description: Key components required to make a deed legally valid.
learning_objectives: List the essential elements of a deed including grantor, grantee, and consideration.
Explain the habendum clause, acknowledgment, and the delivery and acceptance process.

topics.title: Forms of Deeds
topics.description: Full covenant and warranty deeds, bargain and sale deeds, and quit claim deeds.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between various forms of deeds such as full covenant, bargain and sale, and quit claim deeds.
Understand specific covenants like seizin, quiet enjoyment, and further assurance.

topics.title: Conveyance After Death
topics.description: Intestate and testate succession and conveyance procedures.
learning_objectives: Compare intestate succession (title by descent) with testate succession (probate, devise).
Identify the roles of an administrator and an executor.

topics.title: Title Closing Introduction
topics.description: The significance of the closing and the functions of attending parties.
learning_objectives: Explain the significance of a title closing and why it is important.
Describe the functions of the individuals who attend a closing.

topics.title: The Title Closing Process
topics.description: Consummation of the transaction, recording acts, abstract of title, and title insurance.
learning_objectives: Outline the steps in the consummation of a real estate transaction.
Understand the significance of recording acts, chain of title, and title insurance.

topics.title: Closing Costs and Adjustments
topics.description: Analysis of the seller's and purchaser's closing costs.
learning_objectives: List typical seller's closing costs, such as transfer taxes and broker's commission.
List typical purchaser's closing costs, including appraisal, inspections, and title insurance.

topics.title: RESPA and Prorations
topics.description: Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and the arithmetic of prorations.
learning_objectives: Define the basic requirements of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA).
Calculate basic adjustments and prorations for real estate taxes, insurance, and utilities.

sections.title: The Contract of Sales and Leases
sections.description: Principles and procedures related to real estate leases, contracts, and preparation.

topics.title: Types of Leasehold Estates
topics.description: Defining leases and the different types of leasehold estates.
learning_objectives: Define a lease and outline its primary functions.
Identify the four major types of leasehold estates.

topics.title: Standard Lease Provisions
topics.description: The requirements for making a valid lease under contract law.
learning_objectives: List the necessary provisions for a valid lease, such as capacity to contract, demising clause, and consideration.
Understand the plain language requirements for residential leases.

topics.title: Commercial and Residential Leases
topics.description: Distinctions between commercial and residential lease rules, including ETPA.
learning_objectives: Identify key commercial lease elements, such as renewals and right limitations.
Explain residential lease aspects like ETPA rules, luxury decontrol, and security deposits.

topics.title: Essentials of a Valid Contract
topics.description: Basic contract types and essential components.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between expressed, implied, bilateral, and unilateral contracts.
List the essentials of a valid contract, including mutual agreement and consideration.

topics.title: Sales Contract Provisions
topics.description: Detailed clauses and provisions found in a real estate sales contract.
learning_objectives: Identify key components of a sales contract such as legal description, terms of payment, and 'subject to' provisions.
Explain loan contingency clauses, condition of property, and merger clauses.

topics.title: Binders and Purchase Offers
topics.description: The use of binders, purchase offers, and the Statute of Frauds.
learning_objectives: Understand the use and limitations of binders and purchase offers.
Explain how the Statute of Frauds applies to real estate contracts.

topics.title: Contract Preparation Data
topics.description: Gathering necessary data for contract preparation and property descriptions.
learning_objectives: List the documentation required to prepare a contract, including prior deeds, surveys, and tax bills.
Verify property descriptions using metes and bounds, lot on filed map, or street addresses.

topics.title: Mortgage Contingency and Down Payment
topics.description: Types of mortgage contingencies and handling the down payment.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between traditional, VA/FHA, purchase money, and assumable mortgages in contingency clauses.
Understand New York customs regarding the holding and handling of contract down payments.

topics.title: Customs and Contract Procedures
topics.description: Contract signing procedures, sequence, and attorney review clauses.
learning_objectives: Determine who signs a contract first and the reasons behind the procedure.
Explain the role of riders, lead-based paint clauses, and attorney review clauses in the signing process.

sections.title: Real Estate Finance
sections.description: Analysis of mortgage principles, loan types, financing laws, and predatory lending.

topics.title: Mortgage Definition and Mechanics
topics.description: Basic definition of a mortgage, rights retained by owners, and foreclosure.
learning_objectives: Explain how a mortgage creates a lien while the owner retains usual rights of ownership.
Understand basic concepts like bond/note, PITI, foreclosure, and discount points.

topics.title: Duties of a Mortgagor
topics.description: The legal responsibilities and duties of a property mortgagor.
learning_objectives: List the primary duties of a mortgagor, including keeping property repaired and insured.
Understand the requirement to pay all taxes, assessments, and the debt insured.

topics.title: Sale of Property with a Mortgage
topics.description: Scenarios regarding the sale of mortgaged property and recording.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between a sale free and clear, sale subject to the mortgage, and buyer assuming the debt.
Explain the importance of recording a mortgage.

topics.title: General Types of Mortgages
topics.description: Comparison of conventional versus government insured or guaranteed mortgages.
learning_objectives: Identify the characteristics of a conventional mortgage.
Describe the roles of government-backed loans such as FHA, VA, RHS, and SONYMA.

topics.title: Specific Loans and Payment
topics.description: Detailed look at specific loan structures including ARMs, balloons, and wraparounds.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between fixed rate, adjustable rate, balloon, and graduated payment loans.
Understand specialized loans such as blanket, wraparound, swing/bridge, construction, and reverse mortgages.

topics.title: Secondary Markets
topics.description: Overview of Fannie Mae, Ginnie Mae, and Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation.
learning_objectives: Explain the function of the secondary mortgage market.
Identify the roles of FNMA, GNMA, and FHLMC in providing liquidity.

topics.title: Truth in Lending (Regulation Z)
topics.description: Consumer Credit Protection Act, disclosures, and right to rescind.
learning_objectives: Outline the disclosure requirements under the Consumer Credit Protection Act (Regulation Z).
Explain advertising regulations and the borrower's right to rescind.

topics.title: Lender's Criteria for Granting a Loan
topics.description: How lenders assess investment quality of property and borrower ability to repay.
learning_objectives: Determine how lenders evaluate the investment quality of a property via appraisal, condition, and loan-to-value.
Identify the criteria used to verify a borrower's ability to repay, including qualifying ratios and employment history.

topics.title: Predatory Lending Overview
topics.description: Defining predatory lending, sub-prime loans, and associated fraud.
learning_objectives: Define predatory lending and identify practices like hidden fees, flipping, and unwarranted balloon payments.
Understand the impact of predatory lending practices on foreclosure rates and applicable laws.

sections.title: Land Use Regulations
sections.description: Zoning laws, planning boards, and police powers affecting property use.

topics.title: Bundle of Rights and Planning
topics.description: As of right zoning, the need to plan, and private land use controls.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of 'As of Right Zoning' and future planning necessities.
Explain private land use controls such as deed restrictions and covenants.

topics.title: Police Power and Building Codes
topics.description: State and local authority over zoning and building codes.
learning_objectives: Describe how police power is used to enforce Article 9-A and State Building/Fire Codes.
Understand local authority functions like clustering, incentive zoning, and the Sunshine Law.

topics.title: The Planning Board
topics.description: Role of the planning board in managing comprehensive plans and subdivisions.
learning_objectives: Identify the components of a Master Comprehensive Plan and official maps.
Explain the planning board's role in subdivisions, capital budgeting, and PUDs.

topics.title: Zoning Board of Appeals
topics.description: Administrative appeal mechanisms and variance approvals.
learning_objectives: Explain the quasi-judicial function of the zoning board of appeals.
Differentiate between an Article 78 procedure and a special use permit.

topics.title: Use and Area Variances
topics.description: Criteria for granting use ('Unnecessary Hardship') and area ('Practical Difficulty') variances.
learning_objectives: List the criteria required to prove 'Unnecessary Hardship' for a use variance.
List the criteria required to prove 'Practical Difficulty' for an area variance.

topics.title: Local Enforcement
topics.description: The roles of building departments, health boards, and local courts.
learning_objectives: Identify the enforcement responsibilities of building inspectors and code enforcement officers.
Understand the roles of the Board of Health and professional engineering services.

sections.title: Construction and Environmental Issues
sections.description: Fundamentals of building construction systems and environmental hazard management.

topics.title: Land Use and Site Requirements
topics.description: Building permits, codes, and specific on-site requirements in NY.
learning_objectives: Explain the necessity of plans, specifications, and certificates of occupancy.
Describe New York State requirements for on-site wells, sanitary systems, and energy codes.

topics.title: Structural Components and Framing
topics.description: Basic building structural parts from footings to the roof ridge.
learning_objectives: Identify basic structural components such as footings, foundation walls, and wood framing members.
Understand terminology like girders, studs, roof rafters, and slab-on-grade construction.

topics.title: Structural Tie-Ins and Energy Efficiencies
topics.description: Integration of components, R-factors, and insulation types.
learning_objectives: Define structural tie-in terminology including headers, bearing walls, and lintels.
Explain energy efficiency concepts such as R-factor, installation types, and proper insulation placement.

topics.title: Heating and Ventilation Systems
topics.description: Types of heating systems, ventilation importance, and oil tanks.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between hot water, steam, and forced warm air heating systems.
Describe DEC regulations on underground and above ground oil storage tanks.

topics.title: Plumbing Systems
topics.description: Hot water heating, sanitary waste systems, and pipe materials.
learning_objectives: Explain hot water heater sizing and domestic water incoming services.
Compare the advantages and disadvantages of piping materials like galvanized steel, copper, and PVC.

topics.title: Electrical Systems
topics.description: Voltage, amperage, wiring materials, and National Electric Code.
learning_objectives: Identify utility versus landowner responsibilities for electrical service and voltage identification (110/220).
Compare wiring materials such as BX, Romex, Conduit, and Greenfield, and identify panel distributions.

topics.title: Long Standing Environmental Issues
topics.description: Familiar environmental issues including water systems, septics, and termites.
learning_objectives: Recognize public and private water supply testing and health-related issues.
Understand requirements for private sewerage disposal (septics) and NYSDEC wood destroying insect testing.

topics.title: Contemporary Environmental Issues
topics.description: Handling asbestos, lead, radon, and PCBs in real estate.
learning_objectives: Outline health effects and testing procedures for asbestos, lead, and radon.
Explain remediation requirements and disclosure policies for lead and indoor air quality.

topics.title: Environmental Assessments
topics.description: Commercial Phase I to IV assessments, CFCs, and underground tanks.
learning_objectives: Identify the four phases of environmental assessment used in commercial transactions.
Describe issues relating to underground storage tanks, electromagnetic fields, and CFCs.

sections.title: Valuation Process and Pricing Properties
sections.description: Appraisals, market value, pricing properties, and comparative market analysis (CMA).

topics.title: Appraisal, Valuation and Evaluation
topics.description: Differences between formal appraisals, general valuations, and evaluations.
learning_objectives: Define appraisal, valuation, and evaluation.
Identify types of value such as market value, investment value, and insurable value.

topics.title: Market Value vs. Price and Cost
topics.description: Distinguishing market value from sales price and actual costs.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between market value, price, and cost.
Categorize direct (hard) costs versus indirect costs in property development.

topics.title: Comparative Market Analysis (CMA)
topics.description: Preparing and properly representing a Comparative Market Analysis.
learning_objectives: Understand why a CMA must never be referred to as an appraisal under the law.
List the steps in preparing a CMA including data collection and listing comparable past sales.

topics.title: Residential Market Analysis and Highest Use
topics.description: Analyzing the residential market and determining highest and best use.
learning_objectives: Identify key factors in residential market analysis such as recent sold properties and current competing properties.
Explain the concept of 'Highest and Best Use' for both vacant and improved properties.

topics.title: Three Approaches to Value
topics.description: The fundamental methodologies used to value property.
learning_objectives: Describe the sales comparison approach.
Explain the basic premises behind the cost approach and the income approach.

sections.title: Human Rights and Fair Housing
sections.description: Federal and New York fair housing laws, anti-discrimination practices, and agent responsibilities.

topics.title: Pressure from Society and Bias
topics.description: Navigating discriminatory requests and the importance of anti-bias training.
learning_objectives: Identify how to respond to landlords, sellers, or buyers making discriminatory requests.
Understand how attitudes and unconscious stereotypes affect decisions in real estate.

topics.title: Brokers and Salesperson's Responsibilities
topics.description: Required Fair Housing policies, training, and documentation.
learning_objectives: List the broker's responsibilities to maintain and post a Fair Housing Policy.
Identify the required salesperson's actions, including the use of Anti-Discrimination Disclosure Forms.

topics.title: Federal Fair Housing Laws
topics.description: Historical development of civil rights acts and federally protected classes.
learning_objectives: Trace the historical development of Fair Housing from the Civil Rights Act of 1866 to the 1988 Amendments.
List the federally protected classes under the Fair Housing Act.

topics.title: New York State Fair Housing Law
topics.description: Additional protected classes under New York State and NYC Human Rights Laws.
learning_objectives: Identify the additional protected classes specific to New York State Law, such as age and marital status.
Understand the expanded protections under the New York City Commission on Human Rights.

topics.title: Types of Discrimination and Exemptions
topics.description: Recognizing illegal practices like steering/blockbusting and knowing valid legal exemptions.
learning_objectives: Define illegal practices such as steering and blockbusting.
Explain recognized exemptions such as senior citizen housing and the two-family exemption.

topics.title: Legal Ramifications and Do Not Solicit Lists
topics.description: Fines, emotional impacts, and rules surrounding Cease and Desist lists.
learning_objectives: Describe the legal ramifications and potential fines for discrimination violations.
Understand compliance rules for Cease and Desist Lists and Non-solicitation Orders.

sections.title: Real Estate Mathematics
sections.description: Calculations regarding commissions, areas, mortgages, and tax rates.

topics.title: Percentages and Commissions
topics.description: Basic percentage calculations applied to real estate commissions, interest, and points.
learning_objectives: Calculate real estate broker commissions.
Compute interest, points, and rates of appreciation or depreciation.

topics.title: Calculating Area and Length
topics.description: Determining square footage, acreage, and price per square foot.
learning_objectives: Calculate area in square feet for regular and irregular lot sizes.
Convert square footage to acres and calculate price per square foot.

topics.title: Mortgage and Tax Rate Calculations
topics.description: Qualifying mortgages and calculating property transfer and recording taxes.
learning_objectives: Calculate basic mortgage qualifying limits based on income.
Calculate real property transfer tax, mortgage recording tax, and annual real property tax.

sections.title: Municipal Agencies
sections.description: Functions and responsibilities of local town, city, and county agencies.

topics.title: Local Councils and Planning Boards
topics.description: Roles of the City/Town Council and Planning Boards in municipalities.
learning_objectives: Identify the legislative functions of the City/Town Council including budgets and tax rates.
Explain the Planning Board's authority over Master Plans and subdivision approvals.

topics.title: Zoning and Building Departments
topics.description: Functions of the ZBA, Architectural Review Board, and Building Department.
learning_objectives: Differentiate the roles of the Zoning Board of Appeals and Architectural Review Board.
Describe the Building Department's role as 'gatekeeper' for construction activity.

topics.title: Assessors, Health, and Historic Commissions
topics.description: Specialized municipal functions like tax assessment, county health, and environmental councils.
learning_objectives: Explain the duties of the Tax Assessor and Receiver of Taxes/Treasurer.
Understand the advisory and approval roles of the County Health Department and Historic Preservation Commissions.

sections.title: Property Insurance
sections.description: Understanding property and liability insurance options and their purpose.

topics.title: Insurance Basics and Homeowner Policy Types
topics.description: The purpose of insurance, policy types, and standard HO policy coverage.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between monoline and package insurance policies.
Identify basic homeowner policy forms, including HO-1, HO-3, HO-4, and HO-6.

topics.title: Insurance Amounts and the Agent's Role
topics.description: Calculating actual cash value, handling insurance issues, and the real estate agent's role.
learning_objectives: Explain the difference between actual cash value and replacement cost.
Describe the agent's role in explaining property insurance purpose, escrow, and costs to a buyer.

sections.title: Licensee Safety
sections.description: Personal safety, cyber security, and liability issues for real estate professionals.

topics.title: Physical Safety and Environment
topics.description: Safety protocols in the office, on the road, and at vacant properties.
learning_objectives: Identify protocols for working alone, meeting unknown individuals, and office security.
List safety concerns and precautions for open houses and vacant home viewings.

topics.title: Cyber Security and Liability
topics.description: Protecting digital information and managing liability risks.
learning_objectives: Understand how to protect client information and mitigate identity theft/cyber security risks.
Explain the legal liabilities regarding third-party acts, incidents, and use of physical force (Penal Code Article 35).

sections.title: Taxes and Assessments
sections.description: Real estate taxation, property assessments, and challenging tax values.

topics.title: Taxation Purpose and Assessed Value
topics.description: Why real estate is taxed and how properties are assessed.
learning_objectives: Explain the reasons municipalities tax land and improvements.
Describe the relationship between assessed value and full/market value, including assessment ratios.

topics.title: Tax Rates and Jurisdictions
topics.description: Calculating tax rates and identifying different taxing bodies.
learning_objectives: Understand how a municipal budget determines the property tax rate.
Identify the various taxing jurisdictions (city, town, village, county) and the equalization process.

topics.title: Exemptions and Protests
topics.description: Tax exemptions, grievance boards, and small claims assessment reviews.
learning_objectives: Identify common property tax exemptions like STAR, veterans, and elderly.
Explain the procedure for protesting residential assessments via the Grievance Board and SCAR.

topics.title: Tax Liens and Agent Responsibilities
topics.description: The consequences of unpaid taxes and the agent's role.
learning_objectives: Describe the process of tax foreclosure and tax sales due to unpaid liens.
Understand the agent's responsibility to accurately calculate and communicate property taxes.

sections.title: Condominiums and Cooperatives
sections.description: Distinctive legal and financial structures of condos, co-ops, and condops.

topics.title: Defining Condominiums and Sponsors
topics.description: Condo definition, by-laws, and the role of the sponsor.
learning_objectives: Define a condominium as real property and explain common by-laws.
Describe the role of the sponsor in new developments and limitations on their control.

topics.title: Condo Purchasing and Closing
topics.description: Offering plans, letters of intent, Certificates of Occupancy, and closing costs.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of an Offering Plan and the use of 'letters of intent' (CPS1 Phase).
List specific closing costs for condos, such as common charges, Mansion Tax, and title insurance.

topics.title: Cooperative Ownership
topics.description: Shares, proprietary leases, and evaluating a cooperative.
learning_objectives: Define a cooperative as owning shares in a corporation linked to a proprietary lease.
Explain key due diligence issues such as reading the board minutes, financials, and underlying mortgages.

topics.title: Co-op Documents and Board Packages
topics.description: Required documentation, board interviews, and financial requirements.
learning_objectives: List the essential documents needed for a co-op sale, including stock certificates and alteration agreements.
Prepare a purchaser for a cooperative board application, interview, and financial parameter assessment.

topics.title: Condops and Subletting Rules
topics.description: Rules affecting co-op subletting, sponsor shares, and defining a Condop.
learning_objectives: Differentiate the rules for primary residency versus subletting in a co-op.
Define a 'condop' and explain the 80/20 rule concerning passive income activities.

sections.title: Commercial and Investment Properties
sections.description: Commercial property valuation, financial analysis, and commercial leasing.

topics.title: Characteristics of Real Property Investments
topics.description: Investment properties: risk, liquidity, and leverage.
learning_objectives: Assess the risk and liquidity profiles of real property investments.
Explain the concept of leveraging in commercial real estate.

topics.title: Investment Properties Types
topics.description: Different commercial property types including office, retail, and manufacturing.
learning_objectives: Identify the defining characteristics of office, mixed-use, retail, and industrial properties.
Differentiate between fee simple and leasehold ownership in commercial contexts.

topics.title: Property Analysis and Terminology
topics.description: Calculating NOI, cash flow, and understanding square footage definitions.
learning_objectives: Calculate Net Operating Income (NOI), Before Tax Cash Flow (BTCF), and After Tax Cash Flow (ATCF).
Differentiate between rentable, useable, and carpetable square footage, including loss factors.

topics.title: Commercial Leases and Clauses
topics.description: Net, gross, office, and retail leases, and essential lease clauses.
learning_objectives: Identify commercial lease types like net lease, gross lease, and percentage lease.
Explain key commercial lease clauses including attornment, estoppel, and subordination/non-disturbance.

topics.title: Lease Escalation Clauses
topics.description: Mechanisms for increasing commercial rent over time.
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of proportionate share, base year, and operating/tax stops.
Explain escalation formulas like Porter's Wage, CPI, and fixed percentage increases.

sections.title: Income Tax Issues in Real Estate Transactions
sections.description: Federal income tax treatments, capital gains, 1031 exchanges, and depreciation.

topics.title: Federal Income Tax Treatment and Capital Gains
topics.description: Tax rules for primary residences and understanding active/passive income.
learning_objectives: Explain permitted deductions and rules for primary residences under the Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997 ($250,000/$500,000 Rule).
Differentiate between short-term and long-term capital gains, and active, passive, and portfolio income.

topics.title: Like-Kind Exchanges (1031 Exchange)
topics.description: Rules, timelines, and eligible properties for 1031 tax-deferred exchanges.
learning_objectives: Identify properties eligible for a 1031 exchange and define concepts like 'boot' and 'qualified intermediary'.
Outline the strict timelines for 1031 exchanges (45-day identification, 180-day closing).

topics.title: Financing Tax Rules and Depreciation
topics.description: Interest deductions, prepayment penalties, and straight-line depreciation rules.
learning_objectives: Explain federal tax rules regarding interest deductions on home acquisition and equity financing.
Calculate straight-line depreciation for residential (27.5 years) and non-residential (39 years) income-producing properties.

sections.title: Mortgage Brokerage
sections.description: Roles and requirements of mortgage brokers versus mortgage bankers.

topics.title: Mortgage Broker Basics
topics.description: Definitions, requirements, and the distinction between brokers and bankers.
learning_objectives: Define the function and requirements of a Mortgage Broker.
Differentiate a Mortgage Broker from a Mortgage Banker.

topics.title: Dual Agency and Transaction Roles
topics.description: Disclosures required when acting as both real estate agent and mortgage broker.
learning_objectives: Explain the dual agency disclosure requirements under the Banking Law.
Describe the overall role of a Mortgage Broker in closing a real estate transaction.

sections.title: Property Management
sections.description: The functions, agreements, and skills required in professional property management.

topics.title: Introduction and Management Agreement
topics.description: What property management entails and how the management agreement functions.
learning_objectives: Define the core functions of a property manager and when a license is required.
Outline the required components of a written management agreement and the general agency created.

topics.title: Property Manager Skills
topics.description: Accounting, building systems, and legal skills necessary for property managers.
learning_objectives: Identify the diverse skill set required of a property manager, including GAP accounting and HVAC knowledge.
Understand the manager's role in landlord-tenant relations and union negotiations.

topics.title: Markets and Owner Obligations
topics.description: Different property markets, reporting obligations, and budgeting.
learning_objectives: Compare management approaches for office, retail, residential, and condominium properties.
Explain the property manager's obligations to the owner, including setting budgets and rent rolls.
